narrative: OMX30 opens Friday with solid structural support: the index trades +9.2% above its 200-day moving average in established bull regime, MACD remains above signal line, and ADX 42.7 signals strong trending conviction with bullish directional bias. However, RSI-14 at 69.06 and Stochastic K/D at 89.6/84.6 indicate overbought conditions requiring caution on mean reversion. Global markets show mixed signals—Dow +1.14% and Asia strength (Nikkei +1.47%, Hang Seng +1.28%) contrast NASDAQ weakness (-0.80%), while precious metals surge (gold +1.98%, copper +1.83%) and oil edges lower. VIX dip to 15.92 suggests contained volatility, but positioning at 52-week highs leaves limited margin for disappointment.
morningBrief: OMX30 likely opens +0.3 to +0.5% tracking positive European and Asian momentum. Key pivot at 3123 (yesterday close); strong technical support 3085 (lower Bollinger band), resistance 3180 (50-MA). Watch Industrials (28% weight) for export headwinds from USD/SEK retreat; Atlas Copco and Volvo may face consolidation. Banks (18%) remain constructive on NIM narrative despite flat yield backdrop—SEB A, Handelsbanken A buyable on dips. OUTPERFORMERS: Boliden (mining +) on copper/gold strength, commodity plays. WATCH: Ericsson/Hexagon for tech sentiment deterioration (NASDAQ negative). Consumer/retail (H&M B) pressured by weak EUR/SEK import dynamics. US Jobless Claims 14:30 CET key catalyst; beat could trigger tactical risk-off and test overbought technicals. Trade cautiously given RSI warning—take early profits into strength.
drivers: European equities broadly positive (DAX +0.58%, Nikkei +1.47%) supporting regional sentiment
Gold +1.98% and copper +1.83% benefiting commodity-linked exporters
OMX30 at 52-week highs with strong ADX 42.7 bullish setup, but RSI 69 flags overbought mean reversion risk
USD/SEK weakness (-0.85%) slightly headwind for export champions; EUR/USD +0.70% supports multi-currency revenues

sectorImpacts.reason: Gold +1.98% and copper +1.83% directly boost commodity revenue. Boliden particularly leveraged to copper upside.
USD/SEK weakness (-0.85%) headwind for export competitiveness, offsetting broader EU positive sentiment. Oil stable supports input costs.
No yield data today, but structural NIM support intact. Neutral rate backdrop maintains favorable lending spreads.
NASDAQ weakness (-0.80%) signals rotation away from growth; sentiment pressure on high-beta names.
Weak EUR/SEK (-0.18%) increases sourcing costs and margin pressure; weaker spending sentiment from equity volatility.
No yield data today limits directional trigger. Remain positioned for eventual rate normalization headwinds.

watchList.reason: Gold +1.98% and copper +1.83% provide direct revenue tailwind. Trading near 52-week highs but commodity strength fundamental.
Large industrials exporter faces USD/SEK headwind (-0.85%) despite broad EU positive sentiment. Monitor FX moves closely.
NASDAQ down -0.80% pressures tech sentiment; high beta at overbought levels (RSI 69) vulnerable to mean reversion.
watchList.catalyst: US Jobless Claims; tech sentiment shift

watchList.reason: Weak EUR/SEK (-0.18%) increases import costs; retail faces spending pressure in risk-off environment.
Banks sector constructive. SEB offers defensive yield + NIM stability. Position for rate stability narrative.

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish data today. US Jobless Claims (initial jobless claims) at 14:30 CET—consensus ~236K. High sensitivity: beat could trigger risk-off and test OMX overbought positioning.
topRisk: Overbought technicals (RSI 69, Stochastic K 89.6) primed for mean reversion. US jobs data surprise could trigger sharp pullback toward 3100-3085 support. Trade tensions (Anthropic/China news) and succession narrative (JPMorgan) add geopolitical/corporate governance uncertainty.
technicalSummary: ADX 42.7 strong with bullish DI bias (trend conviction high)
Bollinger %B 0.90 upper half—price near upper band, squeeze risk
At 52-week highs (+0.0% from 52W high)—limited upside room
